children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: No State or local statute or regulation, or other State or local legal requirement, may prohibit or have the effect of prohibiting the ability of any entity to provide any interstate or intrastate telecommunications service.

children.heading: State regulatory authority
children.label: b State regulatory authority
children.text: Nothing in this section shall affect the ability of a State to impose, on a competitively neutral basis and consistent with , requirements necessary to preserve and advance universal service, protect the public safety and welfare, ensure the continued quality of telecommunications services, and safeguard the rights of consumers.section 254 of this title

children.heading: State and local government authority
children.label: c State and local government authority
children.text: Nothing in this section affects the authority of a State or local government to manage the public rights-of-way or to require fair and reasonable compensation from telecommunications providers, on a competitively neutral and nondiscriminatory basis, for use of public rights-of-way on a nondiscriminatory basis, if the compensation required is publicly disclosed by such government.

children.heading: Preemption
children.label: d Preemption
children.text: If, after notice and an opportunity for public comment, the Commission determines that a State or local government has permitted or imposed any statute, regulation, or legal requirement that violates subsection (a) or (b), the Commission shall preempt the enforcement of such statute, regulation, or legal requirement to the extent necessary to correct such violation or inconsistency.

children.heading: Commercial mobile service providers
children.label: e Commercial mobile service providers
children.text: Nothing in this section shall affect the application of to commercial mobile service providers.section 332(c)(3) of this title

to a service area served by a rural telephone company that has obtained an exemption, suspension, or modification of that effectively prevents a competitor from meeting the requirements of ; andsection 251(c)(4) of this titlesection 214(e)(1) of this title
to a provider of commercial mobile services.
